text: The Dmax modified method for determining lactate threshold has the highest repeatability and predictive value for endurance performance among eight commonly used lactate threshold concepts.
A fixed blood lactate concentration of 4 mmol/L is a practical and nearly equally effective alternative to the Dmax modified method for determining lactate threshold.
Lactate threshold concepts show good to excellent test-retest repeatability across repeated maximal graded exercise tests separated by two weeks.
Intra-subject variability of lactate threshold measurements across repeated tests is relatively low, with coefficients of variation between 3.4% and 8.1% depending on the method used.
Lactate threshold values correlate strongly with 45-minute time trial performance, with Pearson correlations ranging from 0.65 to 0.94 depending on the threshold concept used.
Lactate threshold values correlate moderately to strongly with prolonged climbing performance (Mont Ventoux road race), with Pearson correlations ranging from 0.53 to 0.76.
